tool.name: MAGs-visualization comp-conta
tool.command:
mags-visualization comp-conta --tools '$tools_cond.tools' --mode '$mode_cond.mode' --top_n '$top_n' --format '$format'
#if $tools_cond.tools == 'checkm' --checkm '$tools_cond.checkm' #else if $tools_cond.tools == 'checkm2' --checkm2 '$tools_cond.checkm2' #else --checkm '$tools_cond.checkm' --checkm2 '$tools_cond.checkm2' #end if
#if $mode_cond.mode == "tax" --gtdb '$mode_cond.gtdb' --tax_level '$mode_cond.tax_level' #end if
#if $mode_cond.mode == "meta" --metadata '$mode_cond.metadata' --meta_col '$mode_cond.meta_col' --meta_bin_width '$mode_cond.meta_bin_width' #end if
@FIG_SIZE_COMMAND@
-o outputs
#if $tools_cond.tools in ('checkm', 'both') && mv outputs/*[Cc]heck[Mm].${format} outputs/final_plot_checkm.png #end if #if $tools_cond.tools in ('checkm2', 'both') && mv outputs/*[Cc]heck[Mm]2.${format} outputs/final_plot_checkm2.png #end if

param.name: tools
param.label: Tool(s) to plot
param.help: Select which quality table(s) should be plotted. Choose 'CheckM and CheckM2' to create one plot for each input table.

param.label: CheckM quality table
param.help: Use the unmodified CheckM QA/quality table containing at least the columns 'Bin Id', 'Completeness', and 'Contamination'.

param.label: CheckM2 results
param.help: Use the CheckM2 quality_report.tsv output. The table must contain at least 'Name', 'Completeness', and 'Contamination'.

param.label: CheckM results
param.help: Use the unmodified CheckM QA/quality table containing at least the columns 'Bin Id', 'Completeness', and 'Contamination'.

param.label: CheckM2 results
param.help: Use the CheckM2 quality_report.tsv output. The table must contain at least 'Name', 'Completeness', and 'Contamination'.

param.name: mode
param.label: Plot mode
param.help: Choose how MAGs are colored: quality uses completeness/contamination thresholds; tax uses GTDB classifications at the selected rank; meta uses a selected metadata column.

param.label: GTDB-Tk annotation table
param.help: GTDB-Tk summary/annotation table for tax mode. Use an unmodified table containing 'user_genome' and 'classification'; genome IDs are normalized internally for matching.

param.label: Taxonomic level
param.help: GTDB rank used to color MAGs in tax mode.

param.label: Metadata table
param.help: Tabular metadata with a header. The first column should contain MAG/bin IDs or sample IDs; values are matched after ID normalization.

param.name: meta_col
param.label: Metadata column
param.help: Select the metadata column used for coloring, Catergorical and boolean values are plotted as categories; numeric values are binned.

param.name: meta_bin_width
param.label: Bin width for numeric metadata
param.help: Width of bins used to group numeric metadata values into discrete categories for coloring. For example, a bin with of 5 groups values 0-5, 5-10, 10-15 etc. Smaller values create more categories with finer resolution; larger values group more broadly.

param.label: Top N groups
param.help: Number of most abundant groups to display (taxonomic groups or metadata categories)

tool.help:
**MAGs-visualization: comp-conta**
This tool visualizes MAG quality by plotting genome completeness against contamination using CheckM/ CheckM2 results.
Available modes:
- **quality**: MAGs are colored by quality category based on completeness and contamination thresholds (high, medium, low). This provides an overview of the overall quality distribution of the dataset.
- **tax**: MAGs are colored by taxonomic classification (e.g. phylum) using GTDB annotations. This allows comparison of MAG quality across different taxonomic groups
- **meta**: MAGs are colored based on a user-defined metadata column (e.g. treatment). This shows how MAG quality relates to experimental conditions or sample metadata.
**Input files**
- **CheckM quality table** (``--checkm``): use the unmodified CheckM QA/quality table. The tool uses the MAG identifier plus the ``Completeness`` and ``Contamination`` columns; additional columns are ignored. Required columns: ``Bin Id``, ``Completeness``, ``Contamination``.
Example::
Bin Id Marker lineage # genomes # markers # marker sets 0 1 2 3 4 5+ Completeness Contamination Strain heterogeneity SRR24759597_bin_73064.fasta c__Gammaproteobacteria (UID4387) 965 277 177 10 265 2 0 0 0 96.05 0.85 50.00 SRR24759598_bin_119213.fasta c__Gammaproteobacteria (UID4387) 965 277 177 59 215 3 0 0 0 86.83 1.27 100.00
- **CheckM2 quality report** (``--checkm2``): use the unmodified ``quality_report.tsv`` output from CheckM2. The tool uses the MAG identifier plus the ``Completeness`` and ``Contamination`` columns; additional columns are ignored. Required columns: ``Name``, ``Completeness``, ``Contamination``.
Example::
Name Completeness Contamination Completeness_Model_Used Translation_Table_Used Coding_Density Contig_N50 Average_Gene_Length Genome_Size GC_Content Total_Coding_Sequences Total_Contigs Max_Contig_Length Additional_Notes SRR24759597_bin_73064.fasta 99.57 1.16 Neural Network (Specific Model) 11 0.868 33844 315.7918598660484 2115342 0.34 1941 93 151911 None SRR24759598_bin_119213.fasta 85.98 0.91 Neural Network (Specific Model) 11 0.867 47949 307.840271877655 2504613 0.34 2354 61 195230 None
- **GTDB-Tk annotation table** (``--gtdb``, tax mode only): use a GTDB-Tk summary/annotation table containing ``user_genome`` and ``classification``. The file does not need to be modified. Genome identifiers are normalized internally before matching them to the CheckM or CheckM2 table. The example below shows the relevant columns only; the original GTDB-Tk output may contain additional columns and can be used unmodified.
Example with the relevant columns shown::
user_genome classification SRR24759597_bin_73064.fasta d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota;c__Gammaproteobacteria;o__Enterobacterales;f__Enterobacteriaceae;g__Frischella;s__Frischella perrara SRR24759598_bin_119213.fasta d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota;c__Gammaproteobacteria;o__Enterobacterales;f__Enterobacteriaceae;g__Gilliamella;s__Gilliamella sp945271295
- **Metadata table** (``--metadata``, meta mode only): use a tabular file with a header. The first column should contain MAG/bin identifiers for bin-level metadata, or sample identifiers such as ``Run accession`` for sample-level metadata. The selected metadata column can contain categorical, boolean, or numeric values. Numeric values are grouped using the bin width parameter.
Example::
Run accession Sample accession Sample alias Replicate Condition Experimental condition Infection by Nosema ceranae Chronic exposure to neonicotinoid Treatment with probiotic SRR24759600 SAMN35523847 II3 NT Replicate3 NT infection by N. ceranae then chronic exposure to 1.5 ng/mL thiamethoxam TRUE TRUE FALSE SRR24759601 SAMN35523846 II2 NT Replicate2 NT infection by N. ceranae then chronic exposure to 1.5 ng/mL thiamethoxam TRUE TRUE FALSE
Requirements:
- CheckM / CheckM2 tables - GTDB annotations (tax mode) - Metadata table (meta mode)
**Outputs**:
- **CheckM plot**: created when CheckM is selected. - **CheckM2 plot**: created when CheckM2 is selected.
Each output is a scatter plot of completeness vs contamination with marginal histograms. In tax and meta mode, colors represent the selected taxonomic rank or metadata categories.
**Layout options:** All size and spacing parameters (figure width, height, spacers) are specified in **cm**.
Example output plots are available in the MAGs-visualization use cases with example configurations and parameter choices: https://github.com/usegalaxy-eu/MAGs-visualization/tree/main/use-cases
For a direct example (bee microbiome dataset): https://github.com/usegalaxy-eu/MAGs-visualization/tree/main/use-cases/bee-use-case/plots
